metadata.summary: Five-step accessibility testing: automated scanning, keyboard, screen reader, cognitive checks, documentation. Automated tools catch 30-40% of violations; manual testing required.

content.title: Accessibility Testing Process
content.summary: Five-step accessibility testing process combining automated scanning (axe, WAVE, Lighthouse, Pa11y), manual keyboard testing, screen reader verification, cognitive accessibility checks, and prioritized issue documentation. Automated tools catch ~30-40% of WCAG violations; the rest require structured manual walkthroughs with real AT.

why.p: Automated scanners produce false confidence: a "0 violations" axe report does not equal WCAG conformance. SC 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships), 2.4.6 (Headings and Labels), 3.3.3 (Error Suggestion) require human judgment. Running both axe and Pa11y catches ~15% more issues than either alone because their rule sets diverge. The complete process — automated + keyboard + screen reader + cognitive + documentation — is the minimum evidence trail for ADA Title II and EAA compliance.

list.item: Pre-release WCAG 2.1/2.2 audit on a web product
CI gate for new pages or components — automated scan must pass before merge
Quarterly compliance review for ADA Title II or EAA-bound products
Procurement: generating VPAT/ACR evidence from real test runs
Triage: converting a raw a11y issue dump into a prioritized remediation plan
Design-stage review — use accessibility-first-design (cheaper to fix in Figma than in code)
Native mobile-only flows — axe/Pa11y do not cover XCUITest/Espresso; use platform-specific scanners
XR/spatial interfaces — WCAG does not fully cover them; use spatial-accessibility and W3C XAUR
Quick one-off contrast tweak — run Colour Contrast Analyser directly, not a full audit

rule.statement: Run both axe AND Pa11y on every audit — their rule sets diverge on ~15% of issues; use the union of violations, not the intersection.
rule.rationale: axe-core and Pa11y have different heuristics and catch different subsets of WCAG violations. A scan with only one tool produces a systematically incomplete picture.

example.code:
What automated tools find: - Missing alt text (axe 1.1.1) - Color contrast failures (axe 1.4.3, 1.4.11) - Missing form labels (axe 1.3.1, 4.1.2) - Incorrect heading structure (axe 1.3.1) - Missing language attribute (axe 3.1.1)
What they miss: - Alt text quality (meaningful vs. generic) - Keyboard operability and trap detection - Reading order vs. visual order - Context and user understanding - WCAG 2.2 drag alternatives and redundant entry

antipattern.description: Treating "0 violations" in axe as equivalent to WCAG conformance.
antipattern.reason: axe covers ~30-40% of WCAG criteria. The "incomplete" and "inapplicable" arrays in axe JSON must be inspected; items in "incomplete" mean the tool gave up, not that the criterion passes.

rule.statement: Tab through the entire page without touching the mouse; every interactive element must be reachable, operable, and have a visible focus indicator.
rule.rationale: Screen reader users navigate by keyboard; axe cannot detect keyboard traps, illogical tab order, or missing skip links — these require a human Tab walkthrough.

example.code: Check | How to Test | Pass Condition Tab navigation | Tab through entire page | All interactive elements reachable Focus visible | Watch for focus indicator | Always visible; contrast >= 3:1 Tab order | Tab and note sequence | Matches visual/logical flow Skip links | Tab from top of page | "Skip to main content" appears Keyboard traps | Tab everywhere | Can always Tab out Custom components | Test accordions, tabs, modals | APG patterns followed

rule.statement: Test with at least NVDA+Firefox and VoiceOver+Safari; a pass on one AT+browser combo does not generalize to another.
rule.rationale: Screen readers and browsers diverge on ARIA interpretation. NVDA+JAWS differ on Windows for the same DOM; mobile AT (VoiceOver iOS, TalkBack) have additional gesture-specific behaviors.

list.item: Navigate by headings (H key) — hierarchy correct and present?
Navigate by landmarks (D key in NVDA) — main, nav, header, footer marked?
All images described — meaningful alt text announced?
Forms: label announced on focus; errors announced on invalid submit?
Dynamic content: live region updates announced (ARIA live)?
Can complete key user tasks end-to-end using only AT?
Clear language: plain language, no jargon, Flesch reading ease >= 60.
Consistent navigation: same location throughout.
Error prevention: confirmation before destructive actions.
Error recovery: clear error messages with actionable solutions; non-blaming tone.
Timeouts: warnings before timeout; option to extend or eliminate.
Animation: can be paused; no excessive motion; respects prefers-reduced-motion.

rule.statement: Every a11y bug must be linked to a WCAG criterion number and include a pre-fix screenshot; never close a bug without both.
rule.rationale: Without criterion mapping and evidence, audit drift is undetectable and VPAT/ACR cannot be produced from the issue tracker.

rule.statement: Assign every issue one of four priority levels based on whether it blocks access completely, creates a significant barrier, creates difficulty, or is an enhancement.
rule.rationale: Without explicit triage, all issues compete equally and critical ones are delayed. Priority drives fix timeline, not just backlog ordering.

example.code: Priority | Definition | Fix Target Critical | Blocks access completely (no workaround) | Immediately / hotfix High | Major barrier; workaround is difficult | Current sprint Medium | Creates difficulty; workaround available | Next sprint Low | Enhancement; not blocking any task | Backlog

rule.statement: Run axe + Pa11y on every PR against a known URL; snapshot axe JSON per release and diff against the previous release to detect regressions.
rule.rationale: Without a CI gate, a11y regressions accumulate silently between releases. Diffing JSON identifies whether a new release introduced new violations.

list.item: Tag Playwright/Cypress tests with @a11y-critical for PR CI; run full crawl nightly only.
For SPA: wait for networkidle AND a route-specific selector before scanning to avoid pre-render snapshots.
Maintain an a11y-exemptions.yml with WCAG criterion + justification + expiry date — never silently ignore violations.
Dismiss cookie banners/consent overlays before measurement; log which cookie state was used.

antipattern.description: Accepting "0 axe violations" as compliance without checking the "incomplete" array.
antipattern.reason: Items in the axe "incomplete" array are criteria the scanner could not evaluate (not "passed"). Treating incomplete as pass creates systematic blind spots.

antipattern.description: Running agent "fix until axe passes" loops more than 3 iterations without an AT regression test.
antipattern.reason: Automated fix loops produce ARIA soup that passes axe but breaks real screen readers. Limit auto-fix iterations and require a human screen reader check after each batch of fixes.

section.name: Agentic Pipeline
section.p: Step 1 (automated) and Step 2 (keyboard via Playwright) and Step 5 (triage and doc generation) are agent-driveable. Steps 3 (screen reader) and 4 (cognitive) require human-in-loop — agent prepares scripts and templates, a tester executes. Pipeline: scanner agent → keyboard-bot → triage agent → report writer.

list.item: Scanner agent: run axe + Pa11y, parse JSON, group violations by WCAG criterion with fix recommendation.
Triage agent: deduplicate by selector+rule across tools, map to WCAG 2.2 AA, assign priority per rubric.
Report writer: populate audit-report.md template from triage output; flag untested criteria as "Not Tested", never "Supports".

section.name: Agent Integration
section.p: Drive Steps 1, 2 (keyboard via Playwright), and 5 (issue triage/doc generation) with Claude subagents. Steps 3 (screen reader) and Step 4 (cognitive) require human-in-loop — agent prepares scripts and result templates but a tester executes. Pipeline: scanner agent → keyboard-bot → triage agent → report writer. Persist axe JSON between runs to track regressions.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — wrap remediation as SDD tasks once issues are categorized; quality-gate enforces WCAG criterion check before "done".
General-purpose subagent driven by this methodology — runs axe-core + Pa11y, parses JSON, opens issues with WCAG criterion + code snippet.
A "screen reader script writer" subagent — generates NVDA/VoiceOver test scripts (utterance expectations) for a human tester to execute.

section.name: Prompt Patterns
section.p: You are an a11y-testing agent. Run axe --stdout --tags wcag2aa,wcag22aa $URL, parse JSON, group violations by WCAG criterion, attach the offending HTML snippet (<=20 lines) and a fix recommendation. Output a markdown table sorted by impact (critical → minor). Do NOT mark issues "passed" that axe couldn't test (look at "incomplete" array).

Given this axe JSON + Pa11y JSON, deduplicate by selector+rule, map to WCAG 2.2 AA criterion, assign priority {Critical, High, Medium, Low} per README rubric, and write findings into the Audit Template.

list.item: @axe-core/cli — Headless axe scan, JSON output. npm i -g @axe-core/cli; https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core-npm
pa11y / pa11y-ci — CI-friendly WCAG scanner, sitemap support. npm i -g pa11y pa11y-ci; https://pa11y.org
lighthouse — Audit incl. accessibility category. npm i -g lighthouse
accessibility-insights — MS scanner CLI + tab-stop validator. https://accessibilityinsights.io
axe-playwright / jest-axe — In-test a11y assertions. npm i -D axe-playwright
cypress-axe — Cypress integration. npm i -D cypress-axe
Colour Contrast Analyser — Pixel-sample contrast on any screen. https://www.tpgi.com/color-contrast-checker/
unified-doc-parse-pdf + pac-2024 — PDF a11y validation. https://pdfua.foundation/en/pdf-accessibility-checker-pac/
Deque axe DevTools Pro — SaaS. API + GitHub Action. IGT (Intelligent Guided Tests), bigger rule pack than OSS axe-core.
Siteimprove — SaaS. REST API. Site-wide crawl, monitors regressions, exports VPAT data.
Tenon.io — SaaS. REST API. Pure API a11y scanner — easy for agent loops.
Level Access (formerly eSSENTIAL) — SaaS. API. Enterprise audit + manual testing services.
Stark — Figma plugin / SaaS. Plugin only (no agent API). Use during design phase.
Pa11y Dashboard — OSS. self-hosted, REST. Long-term tracking on multiple URLs.
Microsoft Accessibility Insights — OSS. CLI + extension. Free, includes guided manual checks.
Fable — SaaS (assistive tech user testing). Human-in-loop. Connect to real disabled testers — agent cannot replace this.
Always run BOTH axe AND Pa11y — their rule sets diverge on ~15% of issues; union catches more.
Tag Playwright/Cypress tests with @a11y-critical and run on every PR; full crawl nightly only.
Snapshot axe JSON per release in reports/v<x.y>/axe.json — diff against last release to catch regressions.
For SPA: wait for networkidle AND a route-specific selector before scanning.
Combine automated keyboard test (page.keyboard.press('Tab') loop, capture :focus-visible) with axe to cover focus order.
Keep an "exempt" allowlist in repo (a11y-exemptions.yml) with WCAG criterion + reason + expiry — never silently ignore.
Test with at least two screen readers per platform (e.g., NVDA+JAWS on Windows) — they diverge in ARIA interpretation.
Don't trust "0 violations" — always check axe incomplete and inapplicable arrays; "incomplete" means it gave up, not passed.
LLM-rewritten alt text often invents content — clamp to what's literally visible/in surrounding text; require human approval for images of people, charts, diagrams.
Headless Chrome reports different contrast than real device with OS-level color filters — sanity-check critical UI on hardware.
Agent loop "fix until axe passes" can produce ARIA soup that breaks real screen readers. Limit to <=3 auto-fix iterations and require AT regression test.
Cookie banners/consent overlays distort scan results — dismiss them before measurement, log which cookie state you used.
WCAG 2.2 added 9 new criteria (e.g., 2.5.7 Dragging Movements, 2.5.8 Target Size Min). Pin axe-core >= 4.8 to include them.
Never let an agent close a a11y bug without linking to the WCAG criterion AND attaching pre/post screenshot — drift reviewers cannot audit otherwise.

template.purpose: Accessibility audit report template with executive summary, methodology table, findings by WCAG principle, and issue detail format
Shell script running axe + Pa11y + Lighthouse in sequence, outputting JSON reports and a summary.md
